briefing.headline: Cover water — post-spawn stained conditions reward moving baits that trigger reaction strikes.
briefing.gameplan:
In post-spawn with stained water and stable conditions, moving baits presentations shine.

Wheeler: On lighter jigheads (1/8-3/16 oz) for Mooch Minnow swimbaits, cut off the tail tabs so the bait rolls more on the lighter head — gives more action for fish higher in the water column. On heavier heads (3/8-1/2 oz) keep the tail tabs intact as they won't affect action with the heavier weight

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a minnow-profile trailer (like CrushCity Freeloader) for natural, erratic movement - it adds realism and bulk without impeding the natural balsa-like hunting action of the ChatterBait.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Hackney: 'Fish a 6.8:1 reel with 16-lb fluorocarbon on a medium-heavy spinnerbait rod. The slower gear ratio helps maintain the medium to slow retrieve needed.'

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

KVD: 'Shad spawn happens around 72 degrees, after bass/crappie spawn — Mother Nature helping them recover by providing a feast. Spikes on full moon, lasts 2-4 weeks. Starts in one area of lake — keep moving until you see activity. Most happens at ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable post-spawn conditions split fish between shallow fry-guarders (2-5ft) and transitioning females (5-15ft). Shad spawn activity on seawalls, riprap, and bluff walls concentrates bigger fish shallow at dawn.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
